caption: Slice 0 of the nested atomic order queue (#826): the rear-move composite (about-face, then march) is now driven by a real `Order.children` tree (`Order.active_leaf()`) instead of the old `Order.Phase` field (TURN/MARCH). This is an internal refactor with NO intended behaviour change -- watch the lone Spearmen regiment reverse 180 degrees in place (front rank becoming rear, footprint held), hold a brief REFORM settle (still the pre-existing bare-field hold, unconverted in this slice), then march the rest of the way to the new line, exactly as before. State-dump verified: the turn completes by tick 88, REFORM releases into the tree's MARCH leaf around tick 245, and the unit reaches its destination and settles around tick 935 -- all inside this clip's extended runtime. The lateral-pivot composite (quarter-turn) is converted the same way but isn't shown in this clip -- both are covered by the GUT suite, including a new live-battle regression test for the #517/#518 double-apply race against the tree-based advancement.
